summary: Strongest concrete product and learning mentions surfaced from today's HN front page comments.

items.name: Metiq
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Metiq: a real time 3D globe for 100 public datasets
items.summary: Real-time 3D globe for live public datasets.
items.why_included: Commenters clearly enjoyed exploring it and the creator was actively fixing rough edges in-thread.
items.evidence: Replies praised the satellite views and called out data-quality and mobile-UX issues the author was already addressing.

items.name: Modeloop
items.source_story_title: Show HN: Modeloop – From visual algorithms to microcontroller C code
items.summary: Model-based design tool that generates embedded C code.
items.why_included: The thread read like a real product review, not a demo, with concrete workflow and validation discussion.
items.evidence: Replies compared it with Simulink, LabView, and Matlab, while discussing contracts, simulation, and open-beta positioning.

items.name: Vocabowl
items.source_story_title: How many of the 170k English words do you know?
items.summary: Adaptive vocabulary quiz app for testing word knowledge.
items.why_included: HN comments focused on pacing and progression, which is useful signal for a learning app.
items.evidence: Replies wanted faster leveling, fewer clicks per word, and a clearer path through the word ladder.

items.name: Fractal OS
items.source_story_title: To study how chips work, MIT researchers built their own operating system
items.summary: Specialized OS kernel for low-noise microarchitecture experiments.
items.why_included: Commenters treated it as serious research infrastructure rather than a novelty OS.
items.evidence: Replies said it helps run benchmarks with less OS noise and pointed to the open-source project site and GitHub repo.

items.name: CourtListener
items.summary: Free legal search engine and alerts for court opinions.
items.why_included: The thread explicitly treated it as the obvious public-access layer for court data.
items.evidence: Replies called CourtListener a vital niche service and contrasted it with the broader PACER access problem.

items.name: RECAP
items.summary: Browser extension and archive that shares PACER documents.
items.why_included: HN commenters recommended it as the practical tool for turning paid court filings into public access.
items.evidence: Replies said RECAP saves money, contributes documents to CourtListener, and works as a Firefox/Chrome/Safari extension.

items.name: iTelescope
items.summary: Remote telescope service for Internet astronomy and astrophotography.
items.why_included: Commenters used it as the exact reference model for the article’s business idea.
items.evidence: Replies said iTelescope already does this and debated whether the economics of the model work out.

items.name: Seestar
items.summary: Smart telescope line for backyard deep-sky imaging.
items.why_included: The thread pointed to it as a practical way to image galaxies and nebulae from light-polluted skies.
items.evidence: Replies said small smart telescopes like Seestar can still work in Bortle 9 urban conditions and from rooftops.

items.name: ISOtunes
items.summary: Bluetooth hearing-protection earbuds with audio passthrough.
items.why_included: AirPods commenters recommended it as a safer alternative for loud environments and riding.
items.evidence: Replies explicitly cited ISOtunes alongside comms gear and other earbud options for hearing protection.
